recaps.text: El Higgins is scraping through her senior year at the Scholomance, a school with no teachers that floats in the void and constantly tries to kill its students with maleficaria. Cursed with an affinity for large-scale destruction that she refuses to use, and too poor and unaffiliated to buy any safety, she survives on sheer grit. To her ongoing annoyance, New York's golden boy Orion Lake keeps saving her life, which only makes the other students trust her less. Alone and defensive, she begins the slow, wary work of turning classmates into allies rather than rivals.

As the year grinds on, El starts building a real alliance, most importantly with the artificer Aadhya and the reserved, musically gifted Liu. She lets slip glimpses of just how dangerous her magic could be if she ever stopped holding back, and she and Orion edge from mutual irritation toward a genuine, prickly friendship. Meanwhile the ordinary horrors of the school never let up: monsters lurk in the vents, the plumbing, and the library, and every alliance doubles as a hard negotiation about who will guard whose back when graduation comes.

El, Aadhya, and Liu commit to a shared graduation plan, agreeing to build a weapon together from the remains of a sirenspider. El keeps refusing the easy path of dark magic even when it would instantly solve her problems, and her reputation slowly shifts from feared loner to someone worth allying with. She learns more about how the enclaves hoard power, and about Orion's unusually voracious knack for destroying monsters, which begins to seem less like simple heroism and more like a mystery she can't quite name.

With graduation looming, El chooses to help the whole senior class rather than only the privileged, casting an enormous working that clears the deadly graduation hall of monsters and reveals the terrifying scale of the affinity she has spent her life suppressing. She and her friends survive the year with their alliance sealed for the run at graduation to come. But her hard-won peace is unsettled by a warning that reaches her from her mother back home: keep away from Orion Lake.
